title: Comprehensive Characterization of Clinical Phenotypes and CTG Repeat-Associated Manifestations in Spinocerebellar Ataxia Type 8
note: → 系統描繪 SCA8（ATXN8OS CTG 重複）之臨床表現型與重複長度相關性，屬亞型辨識與病程分層情報。

en.journal: Cerebellum · Yang D et al.
en.note: → Systematically characterises the clinical phenotypes of SCA8 (ATXN8OS CTG repeat) and their relation to repeat length; subtype identification and disease-stratification intelligence.

title: Upstream non-AUG initiation and ribosomal -1 frameshifting in ATXN8OS CTA/CTG repeat-associated translation
journal: Nucleic Acids Res · Sakamoto S 等
tag: SCA8 機制 · RAN 轉譯
note: → 解析 ATXN8OS 重複序列的非 AUG 起始與核糖體移碼機制，屬重複擴增型 SCA 的毒性蛋白生成路徑基礎研究。

en.journal: Nucleic Acids Res · Sakamoto S et al.
en.note: → Dissects non-AUG initiation and ribosomal frameshifting at the ATXN8OS repeat; basic research on how repeat-expansion SCAs generate toxic proteins.

title: A Translational Readiness Checklist for Stem-Cell-Based Trials in Movement Disorders: A Practical Roadmap
journal: J Cent Nerv Syst Dis · Hemeda S 等
tag: 細胞治療試驗設計 · 最相關
note: → 提出幹細胞臨床試驗的「轉譯準備度查核表」（產品特性描述、基線表型調和、疾病別終點選單），並將 SCA 列為目標適應症之一——可對照本社 Ph2 試驗設計與法規論述。

en.journal: J Cent Nerv Syst Dis · Hemeda S et al.
en.note: → Proposes a translational-readiness checklist for stem-cell trials (product characterisation, harmonised baseline phenotyping, disease-specific endpoint menus) and lists SCA among the target movement disorders — a useful benchmark for our Ph2 design and regulatory narrative.

title: Cerebellar cognitive affective syndrome (CCAS) and [18F]-FDG PET findings in spinocerebellar ataxia type SCA27B
note: → 描述 SCA27B 的小腦認知情感症候群與 FDG-PET 代謝變化，補充非運動症狀評估面向。

en.journal: J Neurol · Cros L et al.
en.note: → Describes cerebellar cognitive affective syndrome and FDG-PET metabolic changes in SCA27B, adding to the non-motor assessment picture.

title: Isoform-paralog specificity and tissue-dependent vulnerabilities in neurological disorders
note: → 綜述廣泛表現的致病基因為何只傷特定腦區：ATXN1／ATXN1L 各自偏好與 CIC-L／CIC-S 結合，形成組織依賴的脆弱性——與同期 Capicua 異構物研究互為呼應。

en.journal: Genes Dev · Hernandez-Quijada K et al.
en.note: → Reviews why broadly expressed disease genes damage only specific brain regions: ATXN1 and ATXN1L each prefer CIC-L or CIC-S, creating tissue-dependent vulnerability — a companion to the Capicua isoform work published alongside it.

title: Genotypic features of Spinocerebellar Ataxia in Northern China: A Comparative Analysis with Southern China
tag: 亞洲流病 · 亞型分布
note: → 比較中國南北方 SCA 亞型基因分布差異，可作為亞洲市場（含台灣、日本）亞型規模推估的對照資料。

en.journal: Cerebellum · Chen B et al.
en.note: → Compares SCA subtype genotype distribution between northern and southern China; a reference point for sizing subtypes across Asian markets including Taiwan and Japan.

title: Functional divergence of Capicua isoforms explains differential tissue vulnerability in neurological disease
note: → 闡明 ATXN1（SCA1 致病蛋白）交互作用夥伴 Capicua 的兩種異構物 CIC-L／CIC-S 功能分化：各偏好結合不同 ATXN1 家族成員，可解釋 SCA1 為何選擇性侵犯小腦——屬 SCA1 致病機制與組織脆弱性基礎研究。

en.journal: Genes Dev · Lee H et al.
en.note: → Shows how the two isoforms of Capicua (CIC-L / CIC-S), an interaction partner of ATXN1 (the SCA1 disease protein), diverge functionally — each preferring different ATXN1-family members, which helps explain why SCA1 selectively strikes the cerebellum. Basic research on SCA1 pathogenesis and tissue vulnerability.

title: Challenges in the diagnosis of spinocerebellar ataxia 27B
note: → 探討新近確認亞型 SCA27B（FGF14 GAA 重複）之診斷困難，屬 SCA 亞型辨識與流病情報。

en.journal: J Neurol Sci · Pons et al.
en.note: → Examines the diagnostic difficulties of the recently confirmed subtype SCA27B (FGF14 GAA repeat); subtype identification and epidemiological intelligence.

title: Safety and preliminary efficacy of autologous bone marrow-derived mesenchymal stem cell transplantation in hereditary cerebellar ataxia: phase I/IIa clinical trial
journal: Stem Cell Res Ther · Moghaddam AS 等
tag: MSC 臨床試驗 · 競品動態
note: → 競品訊號：另一組 MSC 細胞療法在遺傳性小腦萎縮症的 Ph1/2a 臨床（自體骨髓 MSC），與本社異體脂肪 MSC 為同機制對手；同時佐證 MSC 治療 SCA 的臨床可行性敘事。

en.journal: Stem Cell Res Ther · Moghaddam AS et al.
en.note: → Competitor signal: another MSC cell therapy in a Ph1/2a trial in hereditary cerebellar ataxia (autologous bone-marrow MSC) — a same-mechanism rival to our allogeneic adipose MSC, while also reinforcing the clinical feasibility narrative for MSC in SCA.

title: NOP56 is essential for mammalian generation and maintenance of multiple central nervous systems, associated with SCA36 pathology
journal: Acta Neuropathol Commun · Matsuzono K 等
note: → 釐清 NOP56 在中樞神經生成與維持的角色及其與 SCA36 病理的關聯，屬 SCA 亞型致病機制基礎研究。

en.journal: Acta Neuropathol Commun · Matsuzono K et al.
en.note: → Clarifies the role of NOP56 in generating and maintaining the central nervous system and its link to SCA36 pathology; basic research on subtype pathogenesis.

title: Mapping the Disrupted Connectome in Spinocerebellar Ataxia Type 3: A Network-Based Statistics Study Identifying Novel Therapeutic Targets for Neuromodulation
journal: CNS Neurosci Ther · Ou L 等
note: → 以網絡統計法定位 SCA3 受損連結體，指出可作為神經調控介入的新治療標的。

en.journal: CNS Neurosci Ther · Ou L et al.
en.note: → Uses network-based statistics to map the disrupted connectome in SCA3 and points to new targets for neuromodulation.

title: Immune Activation and Glial Dysfunction in Spinocerebellar Ataxias: From Cerebellar Landscape to Disease-Driven Mechanisms and Immunomodulation
tag: SCA 神經發炎 · 免疫調節
note: → 綜述 SCA 的免疫活化與膠細胞失能，並探討免疫調節作為治療切入點——直接呼應本社異體 MSC 的抗神經發炎機制敘事。

en.journal: Cerebellum · Vázquez-Mojena Y et al.
en.note: → Reviews immune activation and glial dysfunction in SCA and explores immunomodulation as a therapeutic entry point — directly echoing the anti-neuroinflammatory mechanism narrative of our allogeneic MSC.

title: Gene therapy for spinocerebellar ataxias
journal: Trends in Pharmacological Sciences · Eo H 等
note: → 系統回顧 SCA 基因療法進展（ASO／AAV 等），對應 Vico VO659、AAV 基因療法競爭象限的最新格局盤點。

en.journal: Trends in Pharmacological Sciences · Eo H et al.
en.note: → Systematic review of gene-therapy progress in SCA (ASO, AAV and others); a current stocktake of the quadrant occupied by Vico VO659 and AAV gene therapy.

title: Adipose-Derived MSCs Improve Motor Function and Reduce Neuroinflammation and Mutant Ataxin-3 Levels in SCA3 Mice
journal: Int J Stem Cells · Lu KM, Ong WK, Ting AE 等
tag: MSC 動物實驗 · 最相關
note: → 動物實驗直接支持異體脂肪 MSC（本社機制路線）對 SCA3 的神經保護與運動功能改善證據。

en.journal: Int J Stem Cells · Lu KM, Ong WK, Ting AE et al.
en.note: → Animal data directly supporting neuroprotection and motor improvement in SCA3 from allogeneic adipose MSC — our own mechanistic route.

title: Longitudinal Dynamics of Polyglutamine-Expanded ATXN3 in Biofluids of SCA3
note: → SCA3 生物標記追蹤方法學，與 Vico VO659 等以生物標記為療效指標的試驗設計相關。

en.journal: Movement Disorders
en.note: → Methodology for tracking SCA3 biomarkers, relevant to trials such as Vico VO659 that use biomarkers as efficacy endpoints.

title: Establishment and characterization of three human pluripotent stem cell lines from patients with SCA27B
journal: Stem Cell Research · Pellerin D 等
note: → 建立 SCA27B 病人來源 iPSC 細胞株，作為疾病建模與療法測試平台（幹細胞領域研究工具進展）。

en.journal: Stem Cell Research · Pellerin D et al.
en.note: → Establishes patient-derived iPSC lines for SCA27B as a platform for disease modelling and therapy testing (a research-tool advance in the stem-cell field).

title: Optimizing twin prime editing components for scalable genome editing and therapy in SCA3
journal: Mol Ther Nucleic Acids · Gwon LW 等
note: → 針對 ATXN3 的 twin prime editing 基因編輯優化，屬基因層級療法路線，與 Vico VO659（ASO）、AAV 基因療法同一競爭象限。

en.journal: Mol Ther Nucleic Acids · Gwon LW et al.
en.note: → Optimises twin prime editing against ATXN3; a gene-level therapeutic route in the same competitive quadrant as Vico VO659 (ASO) and AAV gene therapy.

title: Genetic landscape of SCA in Taiwan（876 例）

en.title: Genetic landscape of SCA in Taiwan (876 cases)
en.journal: Parkinsonism Relat Disord

title: A Review of Spinocerebellar Ataxias in Taiwan

en.journal: Acta Neurol Taiwan

title: The Potential of MSCs in Treating SCA: Advances and Future Directions
note: → MSC 治療 SCA 的最新綜述，直接支撐本社機制敘事。

en.journal: Biomedicines
en.note: → The most recent review of MSC therapy in SCA; directly supports our mechanism narrative.

title: SCA3 Pathophysiology — Implications for Translational Research

en.journal: Int J Mol Sci

title: Cell-based therapeutic strategies for treatment of SCAs: an update

en.journal: Neural Regen Res

title: Stemchymal® Phase I/IIa Clinical Study
tag: 本社臨床 · 基礎文獻

en.journal: Cell Transplant
